name: api
context: Registry API contract - discovery, bundle, retrieval, knowledge, agent, and publish routes for the Dojo server
info: The registry API is the concrete HTTP contract between agents, CLIs, UIs, and the registry server. It includes registry metadata, discovery and search routes, grouped agent-friendly discovery, URI-based retrieval, bundled skill export, knowledge graph reads, agent-oriented recommendation endpoints, and the authenticated publish mutation.
triggers: how do i query the dojo registry
how do i publish to the dojo registry
what does dojo agent ask return
body:
# Dojo API

The registry API is the standard contract between agents, CLIs, UIs, and the registry server. It is intentionally small, but it is not only a search API: it exposes registry metadata, URI-based retrieval, grouped discovery, ecosystem trees, bundled skill export, knowledge reads, graph traversal, alias resolution, agent-oriented recommendation flows, and the authenticated publish mutation.

Use [[dojo/api/query#operation-matrix]] if you want a scriptable client that wraps the route map. Use [[dojo/skill#bundle-workflow]] when you want the portable Dojo skill package itself. Use [[dojo/spec#manifest-core]] and [[dojo/spec#knowledge-fields]] when you need to understand why the payloads look the way they do. The server implementation in `server/src/server.js` and the integration assertions in `tests/integration.test.js` are the runtime source of truth for this standard.

sections.title: Registry index
sections.body: `GET /v1` returns top-level registry metadata such as the registry name, version, loaded ecosystems, and total node counts. This is the fastest route for clients that need a heartbeat plus discovery bootstrap data before calling more targeted endpoints.

sections.title: Discovery routes
sections.body: Use `GET /v1/resolve` for natural-language intent resolution, `GET /v1/search` for broader full-text discovery, `GET /v1/discover` when an agent wants a grouped `learn_first` versus `then_do` answer, and `GET /v1/ecosystems` when a client wants the loaded roots first. `resolve` is the recommendation-style entry point; `search` is the wider search surface with ecosystem, type, tag, limit, offset, `mode`, and `executable` filters; `discover` is the orchestration shortcut for agents that want ranked reading paths and executable follow-ups in one call. These routes now expose `query_variants` and `routes` so agents can see how the server expanded short phrasing like `tx` into richer search terms and then pivot directly into follow-up endpoints.

sections.title: Skill and tree routes
sections.body: Use `GET /v1/skills/*` to load a node plus its `ancestors` and `children`. Use `GET /v1/tree/:ecosystem` when a client wants the nested hierarchy rooted at an ecosystem. The tree route also accepts `depth`, which is useful for UIs that want a shallow first paint and deeper on-demand expansion.

sections.title: Knowledge routes
sections.body: Use `GET /v1/learn/*` for the full knowledge payload, `GET /v1/backlinks/*` for inbound references, `GET /v1/graph/*` for local graph traversal, and `GET /v1/alias/:alias` for natural-language alias resolution. `learn` returns body, sections, aliases, frontmatter, graph edges, ancestry, children, and a computed `reading_path` that turns the knowledge model in [[dojo/knowledge]] into an API payload. It also accepts `question` so agents can ask for `relevant_sections` within a known node instead of rerunning a broader search.

sections.title: Bundle route
sections.body: Use `GET /v1/bundle/*` when an agent needs the portable source package for a node rather than only the manifest. The response includes the manifest envelope plus bundled files such as `SKILL.md`, `agents/openai.yaml`, references, scripts, tests, and docs when they exist. This is the route that lets any client fetch executable skills and knowledge with enough context to reuse them outside the registry UI.

sections.title: Agent routes
sections.body: `POST /v1/agent/ask` recommends executable nodes, explains why they matched, includes an excerpt when one is available, and reports missing required env vars plus required input fields from the selected scripts. `POST /v1/agent/learn` prefers context-rich answers, returns relevant section IDs when it can, accepts `current_context` for local graph biasing, and suggests `then_do` follow-up skills. Together they form the do-path and learn-path for agents.

sections.title: Response shapes
sections.body: The API uses a few stable response patterns. Retrieval routes often wrap the manifest in a `skill` or `node` object plus structural context like `ancestors`, `children`, `links`, or `backlinks`, and now add `execution`, `knowledge`, and `routes` summaries for agents. Search-style routes return ranked arrays plus totals, `reasons`, `excerpt`, `query_variants`, and the same execution-oriented metadata. `discover` groups the same data into `best_match`, `learn_first`, `then_do`, and `alternatives`. `learn` adds `focused_section` and optional `relevant_sections`, `bundle` returns `entrypoints` plus portable files, and `agent` routes add recommendation-specific fields like `alternatives`, `answer_nodes`, or `then_do`.

sections.title: Publish route
sections.body: `POST /v1/skills` is the authenticated write path. The request body is the manifest itself and the bearer token travels in the `Authorization` header. Pair it with [[dojo/validate#tree-aware-checks]] before the request and [[dojo/publish/registry#request-shape]] when you are ready to release.
